title: Server and Client Boundaries

learningObjectives.text: Explain the `use client` boundary using a new example and justify the result.
Apply composition across boundaries using a new example and justify the result.
Explain sharing data and state using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Server and Client Boundaries is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Server and Client Architecture** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **The `use client` boundary** → **Composition across boundaries** → **Sharing data and state**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: The `use client` boundary
sections.body: The directive marks a module as a client entry boundary, including its imported graph in the client bundle.

sections.title: Composition across boundaries
sections.body: Server Components can render Client Components and pass serializable data or server-rendered children across the boundary.

sections.title: Sharing data and state
sections.body: Use props for server-to-client data, URLs for shareable navigation state, server mutations for persistence, and client state for interaction.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: A narrow client boundary owns interaction while surrounding content stays server-rendered.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.
summary: Server and Client Boundaries connects The `use client` boundary, Composition across boundaries, Sharing data and state.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: Which server/client boundary rule is violated?

choices.label: Server Components cannot render Client Components.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Server and Client Boundaries rule and the observable result.

choices.label: A normal server-defined function is being passed to a Client Component, but client props must be serializable unless the function is an explicitly supported server action.
choices.feedback: A normal server-defined function is being passed to a Client Component, but client props must be serializable unless the function is an explicitly supported server action.

choices.label: Strings and numbers cannot cross the boundary.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Server and Client Boundaries rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Client Components automatically download every server closure.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Server and Client Boundaries rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: A normal server-defined function is being passed to a Client Component, but client props must be serializable unless the function is an explicitly supported server action.
questions.hint: Trace the actual values, references, call form, render cycle, request flow, or server/client boundary before choosing.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
